chapter_summary: Jesus teaches about giving, praying (the Lord's Prayer), fasting, trusting God rather than worrying about money, and seeking first God's kingdom.
main_lesson: God sees your heart — give, pray, and trust Him quietly rather than for show.
memory_verse: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
parent_guide: The Lord's Prayer is in vv9-13. Jesus also addresses anxiety directly in vv25-34 — perfect for children who worry. The birds and lilies (vv26-28) are wonderful illustrations of God's care.
application_for_children: You can talk to God every day. He already knows what you need and He promises to take care of you.

verses.kjv: Take heed that ye do not your alms before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven.
verses.little_bible: Be careful not to do good things just so that other people will see you and be impressed — if you do, you won't get any reward from God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't do good things just to show off — do them for God.
verses.meaning: Jesus is concerned with the heart behind our actions. Doing the right thing for the wrong reason — to impress people — empties it of spiritual value.
verses.memory_phrase: Do good for God, not for show.
verses.prayer: God, help me do good things because I love You, not to impress people.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between doing something good and doing something good to be seen?
verses.family_discussion: How can we check our hearts to make sure we are helping others for the right reasons?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today without telling anyone about it.
verses.action_challenge: Secret good deed challenge: do three kind things this week without letting anyone know it was you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures side by side — one giving loudly with a crowd watching, one giving quietly and humbly — God's eyes on the humble giver, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Therefore when thou doest thine alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
verses.little_bible: When you give to someone in need, don't make a big announcement like the hypocrites do — they want people to praise them. And that is all the reward they will ever get.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't show off when you give — those who do have already had their reward.
verses.meaning: A hypocrite is someone who performs religion for an audience. Their applause from people is the only reward they get — God will not add to it.
verses.memory_phrase: Giving quietly honours God.
verses.prayer: God, I want Your approval more than anyone else's. Help me give to please You.
verses.discussion_question: What does 'hypocrite' mean?
verses.family_discussion: How can we give as a family in a way that is humble and quiet?
verses.do_it_today: Give something anonymously today — money, a note, or a gift.
verses.action_challenge: Give secretly to someone who needs it this week. Tell only God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person blowing a trumpet in the street while dropping coins, a crowd applauding — contrasted with a shadowed figure quietly placing coins in a hand, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:
verses.little_bible: But when you give, be so quiet about it that even your left hand doesn't know what your right hand is doing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Give so quietly that even your own hand doesn't know about it.
verses.meaning: This is a vivid picture of total secrecy in giving. The point: your generosity should be so private that you don't even dwell on it yourself.
verses.memory_phrase: Give in secret.
verses.prayer: God, help me be so humble in giving that I don't even dwell on what I've done.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think Jesus means when He says the left hand shouldn't know what the right hand is doing?
verses.family_discussion: Why does God want us to give quietly, without drawing attention?
verses.do_it_today: Give something to someone today — and then forget about it.
verses.action_challenge: Think of the last time you gave something. Did you keep it quiet?
verses.illustration_prompt: A hand quietly slipping a gift to another, no audience, just the giver and the receiver — a moment of pure generosity, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: That thine alms may be in secret: and thy Father which seeth in secret himself shall reward thee openly.
verses.little_bible: Give in secret — and your Father, who sees everything done in secret, will reward you openly.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Give in secret and God, who sees, will reward you.
verses.meaning: God sees what is hidden. A reward from God is infinitely better than applause from people. Secret generosity is seen and treasured by Heaven.
verses.memory_phrase: God sees and rewards secret giving.
verses.prayer: God, I want my giving to be between You and me. Thank You that You see and reward.
verses.discussion_question: What is the reward God gives — what do you think it might be?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing that God sees everything you do in secret change how you act?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today and tell only God about it in prayer.
verses.action_challenge: Look up a Bible story about someone who gave generously in secret.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person's quiet act of giving lit by a single beam of heavenly light — God watching and smiling from above, no other audience, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites are: for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
verses.little_bible: When you pray, don't be like the hypocrites who love to pray loudly in public to impress people. I promise you — the attention they get is all the reward they will receive.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't pray to impress people — those who do have already had their reward.
verses.meaning: Prayer is a conversation with God, not a performance for people. When we pray to be seen, we have replaced God with an audience.
verses.memory_phrase: Pray to talk to God, not to impress.
verses.prayer: God, help me pray because I love You, not to be seen.
verses.discussion_question: How can you tell the difference between praying to God and praying to sound good?
verses.family_discussion: How does your family pray together in a genuine, heartfelt way?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes praying quietly to God — just the two of you.
verses.action_challenge: Keep a private prayer journal this week — just between you and God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man praying loudly on a busy street corner as people stare and whisper — contrasted with a child praying quietly in a closet, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father which is in secret; and thy Father which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly.
verses.little_bible: Instead, when you pray, go somewhere private, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is hidden. Your Father who sees what is done in secret will reward you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Go somewhere private to pray, and God who sees you will reward you.
verses.meaning: The closet is a picture of intimacy. Genuine prayer is a private relationship — the kind of closeness that happens away from an audience.
verses.memory_phrase: God hears your secret prayers.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You hear me even in the quietest moments.
verses.discussion_question: Do you have a quiet place where you talk to God?
verses.family_discussion: How can everyone in your family create a private space and time to pray?
verses.do_it_today: Find a quiet spot and spend five minutes praying privately to God today.
verses.action_challenge: Set up a 'prayer spot' — a special place where you go to talk to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting in a small cosy room, door closed, head bowed in prayer — a warm glow of God's presence around them, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But when ye pray, use not vain repetitions, as the heathen do: for they think that they will be heard for their much speaking.
verses.little_bible: When you pray, don't just babble the same words over and over like people who don't know God — they think that if they say more words, God will be more likely to listen.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't just repeat empty words in prayer — God doesn't need a lot of words.
verses.meaning: Prayer is not about the length or volume of words — it is about the sincerity of the heart. God is not impressed by quantity; He is moved by genuine trust.
verses.memory_phrase: God hears sincere prayer, not empty words.
verses.prayer: God, teach me to pray sincerely, from my heart.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to pray 'vain repetitions'? Is repeating a prayer always wrong?
verses.family_discussion: How can we keep our prayers fresh and genuine rather than becoming routine?
verses.do_it_today: Pray in your own words today — just talk to God naturally.
verses.action_challenge: Write a prayer entirely in your own words and read it to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person muttering the same words over and over at an idol, then contrasted with a child looking up and speaking simply from the heart, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Be not ye therefore like unto them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of, before ye ask him.
verses.little_bible: Don't be like them — because your Father already knows what you need before you even ask Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God already knows what you need before you ask.
verses.meaning: We don't pray to inform God or convince Him — He already knows. We pray because relationship with Him is itself the blessing.
verses.memory_phrase: God already knows what you need.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You know everything I need. I come to You just to be with You.
verses.discussion_question: If God already knows what we need, why does He want us to ask?
verses.family_discussion: How does prayer change you, even when God already knows your needs?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God your needs today — not because He doesn't know, but because it helps you trust.
verses.action_challenge: Make a list of your needs and then give each one to God in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting with open hands, looking up — a gentle, knowing light above showing God's awareness of everything the child needs, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Pray like this — Our Father in heaven, may Your name be kept holy and honoured above everything.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Pray: Our Father in heaven, may Your name be honoured.
verses.meaning: The Lord's Prayer begins with who God is — our Father, in heaven, and utterly holy. Before we bring our requests, we honour Him. This is the right order.
verses.memory_phrase: Our Father in heaven, holy is Your name.
verses.prayer: Our Father in heaven, I honour Your holy name today.
verses.discussion_question: Why does the prayer start with praising God before asking for anything?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to 'hallow' God's name? How do we do that in daily life?
verses.do_it_today: Begin your prayer today by praising God before you ask for anything.
verses.action_challenge: Learn the Lord's Prayer completely by heart this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child's open hands raised upward, warm light from heaven above, a simple home in the background — the posture of a child speaking to their Father, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven.
verses.little_bible: May Your kingdom come — may everything on earth be done the way You want it, just like it is in heaven.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: May Your kingdom come and Your will be done on earth as in heaven.
verses.meaning: We are to pray that God's rule and perfect will would fill the earth — this is the great mission of the gospel. We align ourselves with God's purposes, not our own.
verses.memory_phrase: Thy kingdom come, Thy will be done.
verses.prayer: God, I want Your will to be done — in my life, my family, and the whole world.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think God's kingdom looks like? What would the world be like if everything was done God's way?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family help God's kingdom come in your neighbourhood this week?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to show you one way His will can be done through you today.
verses.action_challenge: Make a list of ways you can help make your part of the world more like God's kingdom.
verses.illustration_prompt: Heaven glowing with perfect peace above, and earth below gradually being filled with the same light as God's kingdom comes down, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Give us this day our daily bread.
verses.little_bible: Give us today the food we need for today.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Give us today what we need for today.
verses.meaning: We ask for daily provision — not a year's supply, but today's bread. This teaches us to trust God one day at a time, daily dependence, daily gratitude.
verses.memory_phrase: God provides what we need today.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for providing what I need today. I trust You for tomorrow too.
verses.discussion_question: Why does Jesus say 'today's bread' and not 'bread for a long time'?
verses.family_discussion: How has God provided for your family's needs day by day?
verses.do_it_today: Before eating today, genuinely thank God for the food He provided.
verses.action_challenge: Keep a 'daily provision journal' this week — write one thing God provided each day.
verses.illustration_prompt: A simple loaf of bread on a wooden table, morning light streaming in — a picture of daily, humble gratitude for God's provision, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.
verses.little_bible: Forgive us for the wrong things we have done — just as we forgive those who have wronged us.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Forgive us our sins as we forgive others.
verses.meaning: Forgiveness flows both ways — from God to us, and through us to others. We cannot hold onto God's forgiveness while refusing to give forgiveness to others.
verses.memory_phrase: Receive forgiveness. Give forgiveness.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for forgiving me. Help me forgive others completely.
verses.discussion_question: Why does Jesus link receiving God's forgiveness with forgiving others?
verses.family_discussion: Is there someone in your family who needs to give or receive forgiveness right now?
verses.do_it_today: Forgive someone today — even if they haven't asked you to.
verses.action_challenge: Think of someone who has wronged you. Write their name. Pray for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two people facing each other — one extending a hand of forgiveness, a chain between them breaking, light flooding in, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil: For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for ever. Amen.
verses.little_bible: Don't let us be led into temptation — and rescue us from evil. Because the kingdom, the power, and the glory are all Yours, for ever and ever. Amen.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Lead us away from temptation and save us from evil. Yours is the kingdom and power and glory, forever. Amen.
verses.meaning: The prayer ends where it began — with God. His kingdom, His power, His glory. We are dependent creatures who need both deliverance and a reminder of who holds all things.
verses.memory_phrase: Thine is the kingdom, power, and glory.
verses.prayer: God, protect me from temptation and evil. All power and glory are Yours.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be delivered from evil?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family support each other when temptation or difficult times come?
verses.do_it_today: Pray the Lord's Prayer slowly today, thinking about every line.
verses.action_challenge: Recite the entire Lord's Prayer from memory without looking.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing in a bright circle of light with darkness around the edges retreating — God's protective presence close, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you:
verses.little_bible: If you forgive other people when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If you forgive others, your heavenly Father will forgive you.
verses.meaning: Forgiveness is not optional for a follower of Jesus — it is the mark of someone who has themselves been forgiven. Forgiveness received must become forgiveness given.
verses.memory_phrase: Forgive others as God forgives you.
verses.prayer: God, because You have forgiven me so much, help me forgive freely.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it hard to forgive sometimes?
verses.family_discussion: How does remembering how much God has forgiven us make it easier to forgive others?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to forgive someone today — tell God about it in prayer.
verses.action_challenge: Read the parable of the unforgiving servant (Matthew 18:21-35) together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Open hands releasing something — a symbol of forgiveness freely given, light streaming upward, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
verses.little_bible: But if you refuse to forgive others, then your Father will not forgive your sins either.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If you do not forgive others, God will not forgive you.
verses.meaning: Jesus makes this stark because the stakes are real. A heart closed to forgiving others reveals it has not truly grasped its own need for forgiveness.
verses.memory_phrase: Unforgiveness blocks our own forgiveness.
verses.prayer: God, reveal any unforgiveness in my heart. I want to be free.
verses.discussion_question: Is there anyone you have refused to forgive? What can you do about it?
verses.family_discussion: How does holding grudges affect a family? How does forgiveness heal it?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to search your heart for any unforgiveness. Deal with it today.
verses.action_challenge: Write a letter of forgiveness to someone — whether you send it or not is up to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person with arms crossed, a heavy chain visible — contrasted with someone arms open, chain broken, standing in freedom, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
verses.little_bible: When you fast, don't put on a gloomy face like the hypocrites do — they make themselves look terrible so that everyone knows they are fasting. I tell you truly, that attention is all the reward they will get.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When you fast, don't look miserable to show off. Those who do have had their reward.
verses.meaning: Even fasting — a deeply serious spiritual practice — can become a performance. God does not want religious suffering for show; He wants sincere hearts.
verses.memory_phrase: Fast for God, not for attention.
verses.prayer: God, when I sacrifice for You, help me do it with a joyful heart, not for show.
verses.discussion_question: What is fasting? Why do people do it?
verses.family_discussion: How can we practice self-discipline and sacrifice in a way that is genuine and not showy?
verses.do_it_today: Do something for God today that no one else will see.
verses.action_challenge: Find out what the Bible says about fasting and discuss it as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person with exaggerated sad expression and messy hair, performing misery for a crowd — contrasted with someone fasting peacefully with a clean, calm face, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face;
verses.little_bible: But when you fast, wash your face and fix your hair — look normal.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When you fast, look normal — wash your face and brush your hair.
verses.meaning: Looking normal while fasting is the outward sign of an inward reality — your sacrifice is between you and God, no audience needed.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep your fasting between you and God.
verses.prayer: God, I want my sacrifice to be real — just between us.
verses.discussion_question: Why does Jesus say to look clean and normal when fasting?
verses.family_discussion: What kinds of sacrifice can your family do quietly, just for God?
verses.do_it_today: Give up something today — screen time, a snack — quietly, just for God.
verses.action_challenge: Try a small fast this week — maybe skipping a meal or a treat — and spend that time praying.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person going about their day normally — clean, cheerful — but with a quiet inner devotion to God visible in their peaceful expression, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: That thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father which is in secret: and thy Father, which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
verses.little_bible: So no one but God knows you are fasting. Your Father who sees what is done in secret will reward you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Fast secretly, and God who sees in secret will reward you.
verses.meaning: The pattern repeats: secret faithfulness, open reward. God's gaze on our private devotion is worth more than any public recognition.
verses.memory_phrase: God rewards secret faithfulness.
verses.prayer: God, You see everything I do quietly for You. Thank You for rewarding what is unseen.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think God's reward for secret faithfulness looks like?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing God sees our private acts of devotion change how we live?
verses.do_it_today: Do one private act of devotion to God today.
verses.action_challenge: Keep a secret devotion journal — recording only things between you and God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A quiet room with a person kneeling in private prayer, a warm beam of light from above representing God's loving attention, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal:
verses.little_bible: Don't spend your life collecting things here on earth, where moths can eat them, rust can destroy them, and thieves can steal them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't store up treasures on earth where they can be ruined or stolen.
verses.meaning: Earthly treasures are temporary — they all decay, break, or get taken. Investing your life in them is investing in things that cannot last.
verses.memory_phrase: Earth's treasures do not last.
verses.prayer: God, help me care more about lasting things than earthly things.
verses.discussion_question: What are some things people treasure on earth that don't last forever?
verses.family_discussion: What is your family most tempted to treasure above God?
verses.do_it_today: Look at one possession you really love. Ask: is this eternal or temporary?
verses.action_challenge: Make two lists: 'earthly treasures' and 'eternal treasures.' Which list is longer?
verses.illustration_prompt: A beautiful golden chest decaying — rust spots forming, moths eating the fabric inside, a crack showing it won't last, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal:
verses.little_bible: Instead, store up treasures in heaven, where nothing can destroy them and no thief can ever steal them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Store up treasures in heaven where nothing can destroy them.
verses.meaning: Heavenly treasure — the fruit of a life lived for God — is permanent, safe, and infinitely more valuable than anything on earth.
verses.memory_phrase: Store treasure in heaven.
verses.prayer: God, help me invest in things that last forever — in people, in Your kingdom, in love.
verses.discussion_question: What kinds of things are 'heavenly treasure'? How do we store them up?
verses.family_discussion: What are your family doing today that will have eternal value?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that will matter in eternity — be kind, pray for someone, share the gospel.
verses.action_challenge: Make a list of five things that have eternal value. Do one this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A gleaming treasure chest in heaven surrounded by golden light — untouchable, indestructible, waiting, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
verses.little_bible: Wherever your treasure is — that is where your heart will be also.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Whatever you treasure most, that is where your heart will be.
verses.meaning: Our hearts follow our investments. What we spend our time, money, and energy on reveals what we truly value.
verses.memory_phrase: Your heart follows your treasure.
verses.prayer: God, help my heart follow You — the most valuable treasure of all.
verses.discussion_question: What do you spend most of your time thinking about? What does that tell you about your heart?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, what do we invest most in? Does that match our values?
verses.do_it_today: Think about what you gave your attention to today. Is it leading your heart to God?
verses.action_challenge: Track how you spend your time tomorrow and see what you are 'investing' in.
verses.illustration_prompt: A heart and a treasure chest linked by a golden thread — the heart moving toward whatever the treasure is, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: The light of the body is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.
verses.little_bible: Your eye is the lamp of your body. If your eyes are focused and healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If your eye is focused on good things, your whole body will be full of light.
verses.meaning: What we look at — what we focus our attention and desire on — determines whether we walk in light or darkness. A single, pure focus on God fills us with His light.
verses.memory_phrase: Focus your eyes on God.
verses.prayer: God, help me keep my eyes focused on You and Your good things.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to have your eye 'focused' or 'single'?
verses.family_discussion: What are we regularly watching or looking at as a family? Does it fill us with light?
verses.do_it_today: Choose carefully what you look at today — does it fill you with light?
verses.action_challenge: Do a 'media fast' from something that doesn't fill you with God's light.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright eye with focused clear vision — light filling the whole body — versus a dim, cloudy eye with shadows around it, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!
verses.little_bible: But if your eye is bad and focused on evil things, your whole body will be full of darkness. If what you think is light is actually darkness — how terribly dark that must be!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If your eye is focused on bad things, your whole body will be full of darkness.
verses.meaning: Choosing wrong things to focus on doesn't just harm us — it corrupts our inner light. What we feed our eyes and minds shapes who we become.
verses.memory_phrase: Guard your eyes — guard your heart.
verses.prayer: God, help me guard my eyes and fill my mind with Your good things.
verses.discussion_question: What kinds of things can fill us with darkness if we look at them too much?
verses.family_discussion: How does your family guard your eyes and minds from things that lead to darkness?
verses.do_it_today: Turn away from something today that you know doesn't bring light.
verses.action_challenge: Find Psalm 101:3 and Philippians 4:8 and talk about what they say about what to look at.
verses.illustration_prompt: A shadowed figure with a dim eye — darkness spreading through them — a visual contrast to the bright figure of verse 22, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
verses.little_bible: No one can serve two masters. You will end up loving one and hating the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You cannot serve both God and money. You must choose.
verses.meaning: Divided loyalties are impossible in the end. Money is not evil, but making it your master is. God alone can be Lord of our lives.
verses.memory_phrase: Choose God, not money, as your master.
verses.prayer: God, I choose You as my master. Help me hold money loosely.
verses.discussion_question: What is 'mammon' and why can't it be our master?
verses.family_discussion: How does money try to become the master of a family? How do you keep God as Lord instead?
verses.do_it_today: Give something away today as a way of saying 'money is not my master.'
verses.action_challenge: Talk about your family's attitude to money. Does it reflect God as master?
verses.illustration_prompt: A person standing at a fork in the road — one path leads to a golden bag of coins, the other leads to a shining cross — they cannot take both paths, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment?
verses.little_bible: That is why I tell you: don't worry about your life — about what you will eat or drink, or about what you will wear. Isn't life more than food? And isn't your body more than clothes?
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't worry about what to eat, drink, or wear. Life is about more than these things.
verses.meaning: Jesus speaks directly to anxiety. Worry about basics — food, drink, clothes — misses the point. Life is far bigger and God is far more attentive than our worries suggest.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't worry — God is bigger than your needs.
verses.prayer: God, I bring You my worries today. Help me trust that You are in control.
verses.discussion_question: What things do you worry about most? Can you give them to God?
verses.family_discussion: How does worry affect your family? How can you help each other trust God instead?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing you are worried about. Give it to God in prayer.
verses.action_challenge: Make a 'worry list' and a 'trust list' — move everything from one to the other.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child lying in a field looking up at the sky, surrounded by food and flowers — completely peaceful, no worry — a picture of life beyond anxiety, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Behold the fowls of the air: for they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?
verses.little_bible: Look at the birds in the sky — they don't plant seeds or harvest crops or store food in barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds every one of them. And you are worth so much more than a bird!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Look at the birds — God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!
verses.meaning: God's care for birds is observable and real — they are fed every day without planting a crop. If God cares for birds, how much more does He care for people made in His image?
verses.memory_phrase: God feeds the birds — He will care for you.
verses.prayer: God, You care for every bird. Thank You that You care for me even more.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever watched birds finding food? What does that show you about God?
verses.family_discussion: How has God provided for your family in unexpected ways?
verses.do_it_today: Watch birds outside and say: 'God feeds them. He cares for me.'
verses.action_challenge: Put out bird seed and watch God's care for His creatures.
verses.illustration_prompt: Colourful birds swooping and eating in a sunny field, the sky bright and generous above them — a picture of God's effortless provision, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?
verses.little_bible: And can any of you make yourself even a little bit taller by worrying?
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Can worrying make you even a tiny bit taller? No!
verses.meaning: Worry is not only unproductive — it is powerless. We cannot change a single thing by worrying. God can; we cannot.
verses.memory_phrase: Worry changes nothing — God changes everything.
verses.prayer: God, remind me that worrying does nothing. You are in control.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of anything that got better because of worrying about it?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between planning wisely and worrying anxiously?
verses.do_it_today: Every time you feel a worry today, say: 'Worry changes nothing. God is in control.'
verses.action_challenge: Make a 'worry jar' — write worries on paper, put them in the jar, give them to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small child trying to stretch themselves taller by pulling on their hair — obviously impossible — a funny image with a serious point, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin:
verses.little_bible: And why worry about clothes? Look at how the wildflowers grow — they don't work or make their own clothing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't worry about clothes — look at the flowers. They don't work, yet they are beautiful.
verses.meaning: The wildflowers do nothing to create their beauty — and yet they are arrayed in splendour. If God clothes plants so beautifully, He will certainly clothe His people.
verses.memory_phrase: God clothes the flowers — He will care for you.
verses.prayer: God, like the flowers You made beautiful, help me trust Your care for me.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most beautiful flower you've ever seen? Who made it?
verses.family_discussion: How does the beauty of creation give us confidence in God's care?
verses.do_it_today: Pick or buy a flower. Look at it carefully and thank God for His artistry.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a wildflower and write beside it: 'God made this beautiful without any worry.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A field of bright wildflowers in bloom — effortlessly beautiful, swaying in the breeze — God's artistry evident in every petal, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And yet I say unto you, That even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
verses.little_bible: And yet I tell you — even Solomon in all his splendid royal robes was not dressed as magnificently as one of these flowers!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Even King Solomon was not as beautiful as one of these flowers.
verses.meaning: The greatest human wealth and splendour cannot match what God creates effortlessly. God's provision exceeds anything human achievement can produce.
verses.memory_phrase: God's creation outshines human glory.
verses.prayer: God, You are more beautiful and magnificent than anything we can make. I worship You.
verses.discussion_question: Who was Solomon and what was he famous for?
verses.family_discussion: What does this verse tell us about the difference between God's work and human work?
verses.do_it_today: Look at something in nature today and say: 'Not even the richest king could make this.'
verses.action_challenge: Research how beautiful Solomon's palace was, and then compare it to a garden.
verses.illustration_prompt: King Solomon in magnificent golden robes standing beside a field of simple wildflowers — the flowers more brilliant, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, but how much more shall he clothe you, O ye of little faith?
verses.little_bible: So if God clothes the grass of the field — grass that is here today and burned tomorrow — how much more will He clothe you? You have so little faith!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If God clothes the grass, He will certainly clothe you. Trust Him!
verses.meaning: Grass is fleeting — burned as fuel. But even it is dressed beautifully by God. If God cares for the temporary, how much more for people made for eternity?
verses.memory_phrase: God cares for you more than the grass.
verses.prayer: God, forgive me for my little faith. Help me trust Your care for me more.
verses.discussion_question: If God takes care of something as temporary as grass, what does that tell us about how He cares for us?
verses.family_discussion: What has made you doubt God's care lately? Talk about it honestly.
verses.do_it_today: Run your hand through some grass and say: 'God dressed even this. He will care for me.'
verses.action_challenge: Write 'O ye of little faith' somewhere you'll see it — as a funny reminder to trust.
verses.illustration_prompt: Green grass growing in sunlight, beautiful but brief — and above it, a warm face of God looking down with love on a small child, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?
verses.little_bible: So don't worry and say: 'What will we eat? What will we drink? What will we wear?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't worry about what to eat, drink, or wear.
verses.meaning: Jesus repeats the command against worry because it is that important. Anxiety about basics is a sign of forgetting who our Father is.
verses.memory_phrase: Do not say: what shall I eat? Trust God.
verses.prayer: God, when anxious thoughts come, help me remember You are my Father who provides.
verses.discussion_question: Why does Jesus repeat this command about worry so many times in this passage?
verses.family_discussion: What basic needs has God always provided for your family?
verses.do_it_today: List five needs God has already met in your life. Praise Him for them.
verses.action_challenge: Make a 'God provided' wall — list all the ways God has provided for your family this year.
verses.illustration_prompt: Three thought bubbles — food, water, clothes — with a big 'X' through them, replaced by a peaceful face looking upward in trust, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: For after all these things do the Gentiles seek: for your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.
verses.little_bible: People who don't know God spend all their energy chasing after these things — but your heavenly Father already knows you need them all.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God knows everything you need. You don't need to worry like those who don't know Him.
verses.meaning: Anxiety about basic needs is what characterises a life without God. But those who know their heavenly Father know that He already sees and knows their needs.
verses.memory_phrase: Your Father knows what you need.
verses.prayer: Heavenly Father, thank You that You know every need before I name it.
verses.discussion_question: How does knowing God as 'Father' change how you feel about your needs?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between how believers and non-believers can approach life's worries?
verses.do_it_today: Pray: 'Father, You know what I need today. I trust You.'
verses.action_challenge: Write a list of your needs and then write beside each one: 'My Father knows.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd rushing about anxiously in the background while in the foreground a peaceful child sits quietly, trusting — the difference between knowing God and not, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
verses.little_bible: Instead, seek God's kingdom and His righteousness above everything else — and all the things you need will be given to you as well.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Seek God's kingdom first, and He will provide everything else.
verses.meaning: This is the great promise and the great priority. Not: get your needs met, then follow God. But: put God first, and He will make sure your needs are met. This is the life of faith.
verses.memory_phrase: Seek God first — He will add the rest.
verses.prayer: God, help me put Your kingdom first every single day.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'seek first the kingdom of God'?
verses.family_discussion: How does your family's daily life show that God's kingdom is your first priority?
verses.do_it_today: Start your day with God today — read the Bible, pray — before anything else.
verses.action_challenge: For one week, spend the first ten minutes of each day with God. See what happens.
verses.illustration_prompt: A compass pointing to 'God's Kingdom' as true north — with everything else (food, clothes, shelter) arranged around it, falling gently into place, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
verses.little_bible: So don't worry about tomorrow — tomorrow will have its own things to deal with. Today has enough to handle already.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't worry about tomorrow. Today has enough for today.
verses.meaning: Jesus calls us to live one day at a time — trusting God with today's challenges and releasing tomorrow into His hands. This is the secret to peace.
verses.memory_phrase: Live one day at a time with God.
verses.prayer: God, I give You tomorrow's worries. Help me live faithfully in today.
verses.discussion_question: What is one worry about tomorrow you can give to God right now?
verses.family_discussion: How does living one day at a time help your family stay close to God?
verses.do_it_today: Write down a tomorrow-worry. Say: 'God, I give this to You.' Tear it up.
verses.action_challenge: At the end of each day this week, write one thing from today you are grateful God handled.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sleeping peacefully with a sunrise through the window — tomorrow is bright and in God's hands — storybook watercolor style.
